definition: The state of being well known for some bad quality or deed
example: Yesterday, December 7, 1941—a date which will live in infamy.

definition: The area around a fireplace; used figuratively to denote domestic comfort
example: His fireside chats brought the president into every American living room.

definition: A long and severe recession in an economy or market
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself in times of depression.

definition: Anxiety or fear that something bad or unpleasant will happen
example: Let me assert my firm belief that the only thing we have to fear is fear itself—nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror which paralyzes needed efforts to convert retreat into advance.

definition: Extreme fear
example: The only thing we have to fear is fear itself—nameless, unreasoning, unjustified terror.

definition: The crime of betraying one's country
example: Economic royalists sought to control the government in a form of treason against the people.

definition: A supporter of the king or the principle of monarchy
example: Economic royalists have carved a new dynasty of power.

definition: The state of being unemployed
example: I see one-third of a nation ill-housed, ill-clad, ill-nourished, suffering from unemployment.

definition: The action or process of making ready or being made ready for use
example: Victory requires preparation and sacrifice.

definition: Not conquerable; that cannot be overcome or defeated
example: The American spirit is unconquerable in the face of any challenge.

definition: The feeling or belief that one can rely on someone or something
example: We have confidence in the future of democracy.

definition: An instance of resuming an activity or state after an interruption
example: Let us dedicate ourselves to a new task and a new renewal of spirit.

definition: Poverty so extreme that one lacks the means to provide for oneself
example: We seek to end the destitution of millions in our land.

definition: Assistance, especially in the form of food, clothing, or money, given to those in special need or difficulty
example: The New Deal brought relief to millions of suffering Americans.

definition: The capacity to recover quickly from difficulties
example: The resilience of the American people shall see us through.

definition: A collection of weapons and military equipment; a place where weapons are made or stored
example: We must be the great arsenal of democracy.

definition: A system of government by the whole population
example: We must be the great arsenal of democracy in the fight against tyranny.

definition: Stop resisting to an enemy or opponent and submit to their authority
example: No surrender to fear shall darken our path.

definition: Something that serves as a sign or evidence of a specified fact
example: Our recovery is a testament to American ingenuity.

definition: A feeling of expectation and desire for a certain thing to happen
example: We have nothing to fear but fear itself, and hope shall guide us through.
